headline: Ukraine CONFIRM at 50.0 / Functional on final close of 3-day US-brokered trilateral ceasefire (May 9-11). Final scoring decision: sustained 3-day breach-free conduct + concrete ACT cooperation (1,000-prisoner list publicly submitted; Zelenskyy 'must take place' framing; US-guaranteed framework engaged) earns upward dimensional pressure on ACT and INT (ACT 3.5 → 4.0; INT 3.0 → 3.5) under the day-3 final-window scoring rule. Reconstructed composite 53.1 (+3.1 from published 50.0). Per methodology v1.2 materiality threshold (5pt floor for change proposals), delta is sub-threshold. CONFIRM at 50.0 with explicit dimensional-upward-pressure note recorded. Rule articulated: sustained breach-free conduct under counterparty-violated ceasefire earns dimensional credit (ACT for concrete cooperative follow-through; INT for consistency-under-pressure across full window) but does NOT automatically translate to material composite movement when the credit produces a sub-threshold composite delta. The ISW May 12 retrospective finding that Russia used the ceasefire as a strategic stockpile window analytically deepens the counterparty bad-faith pattern — it does not directly add to Ukraine's score (Ukraine's conduct credit is earned for its own consistent behavior, not by reference to Russia's behavior). Ceasefire window CLOSED. Next scoring trigger: post-ceasefire posture (June rotation).
ceasefire_window_status: Days 1-2-3 complete. Ukraine no confirmed breach across all 3 days. 1,000-prisoner list submitted; exchange not completed at expiry. Counterparty breached on all 3 days + used ceasefire as stockpile window per ISW. Ukraine's conditional credit is converted to dimensional upward pressure on ACT and INT but produces a sub-threshold composite delta (+3.1).
dimensional_upward_pressure_note: Internal scoring shows ACT 4.0 and INT 3.5 are defensible at day-3 close based on sustained 3-day conduct record. Reconstructed composite at internal scoring would be 53.1 (+3.1 delta from published 50.0). Per methodology v1.2 materiality threshold (5.0pt floor for change-proposal generation), the delta is sub-threshold. Decision: confirm at published 50.0 with the dimensional upward pressure documented for forward reference. If any post-ceasefire negative-signal materializes in the June rotation, the upward pressure is consumed; if positive trajectory continues (e.g., exchange ultimately completes, sustained good-faith posture continues), the +3.1 becomes the floor of a potentially larger upgrade in the next material assessment cycle.

final_ceasefire_scoring_rule_articulated.rule_statement: When a party maintains sustained breach-free conduct across all days of a multi-day ceasefire window in which the counterparty has accumulated documented breaches, the consistent party EARNS dimensional upward credit on ACT (for concrete cooperative-action follow-through during the window) and INT (for consistency-under-pressure across the full window). When the resulting composite delta is below the 5.0pt materiality threshold for change-proposal generation, the published score is CONFIRMED but the dimensional upward pressure is documented as carry-forward for the next material assessment. The party does not receive automatic upward composite movement merely because the counterparty was bad-faith; credit is earned on the consistent party's own conduct evidence, and the materiality threshold protects against churning of small-delta movements.
final_ceasefire_scoring_rule_articulated.application_to_ukraine_2026_05_11: Ukraine maintained breach-free conduct across days 1, 2, and 3 of the trilateral ceasefire. Concrete cooperative-action: 1,000-prisoner list publicly submitted; Zelenskyy 'must take place' framing rebutted Putin's 'not ready' blame-shifting; US-guaranteed framework engaged. Dimensional credit awarded internally: ACT 3.5 → 4.0; INT 3.0 → 3.5. Composite delta +3.1 (sub-threshold). Decision: confirm at 50.0; dimensional upward pressure carried to next material assessment.
final_ceasefire_scoring_rule_articulated.rule_outcome: 50.0 Functional confirmed. Dimensional upward pressure on ACT and INT documented. No change proposal generated (sub-threshold). Ukraine retains 'partial-credit-pending' status for post-ceasefire trajectory observation.

key_evidence: May 11 2026 (in-window, day 3 ceasefire close): No confirmed Ukrainian-attributed breach across day 3. Ukrainian forces continued defensive posture; General Staff reported 38 Russian attacks by 16:00. Ukraine maintained the 3-day breach-free conduct record.

May 11 2026 (in-window): Talks continued on Trump-brokered 1,000-prisoner exchange; Putin claimed Kyiv was 'not ready' for swap; Ukraine publicly disputed (Zelenskyy: list submitted; 'must take place — Americans assumed responsibility for guarantees'). Exchange NOT completed by ceasefire expiry — coordination failure with disputed attribution (Russia frames as Ukrainian unreadiness; Ukraine frames as Russian obstruction). Independent reporting cannot definitively attribute the coordination failure to a single party but Ukraine submitted the list as agreed.

May 11 2026 (in-window, counterparty conduct affecting INT analysis): Russian drones, bombs and artillery struck Kharkiv and Kherson civilian areas — at least 2 killed, 7 wounded (incl. 14-year-old boy). Ukraine's INT credit is earned independently of Russia's conduct but the asymmetry is documented.

May 12 2026 (just outside window, retrospective ISW analysis covering in-window conduct): ISW finding — Russia exploited the Victory Day ceasefire as a STRATEGIC STOCKPILE WINDOW. Russian drone strikes ROSE during the truce (pre-positioned Molniya drones deployed); reserves repositioned forward in the Lyman direction. This converts the bad-faith conduct from individual-breach-level to strategic-format-exploitation level. Ukraine's by-contrast 3-day consistency record is analytically deepened (sustained good-faith conduct under counterparty's strategic bad-faith exploitation).

Cumulative 3-day Ukrainian conduct record (May 9-11): Zero confirmed breach. 1,000-prisoner list submitted. Cooperative posture maintained under counterparty obstruction and blame-shifting. Public communications balanced (Zelenskyy acknowledged both partial Russian compliance and continued breaches across all 3 days).

Cumulative ACT signal: Day-3 final close finds the prisoner exchange in 'list-submitted-but-execution-failed' status. Per scanner assessor note: 'classify as prepared and submitted but not executed; this is a partial ACT credit — Ukraine fulfilled its list-submission obligation; failure to execute attributable to coordination breakdown and Russia's disputed readiness.' This earns ACT dimensional credit (+0.5 internal) but the credit is materially insufficient to produce a material composite upgrade.

evidence.source: PBS Newshour — Russia and Ukraine trade blame for continued fighting that killed at least 2 as ceasefire nears its end
evidence.finding: Day-3 confirmation: Russian strikes killed 2 civilians and wounded 7 (incl. 14-year-old) in Kharkiv/Kherson; ceasefire expired with prisoner exchange incomplete. Ukraine's defensive posture documented; no Ukrainian-attributed breach.

evidence.source: Euromaidan Press — Talks continue on Trump-brokered prisoner exchange after Putin claimed Kyiv was not ready
evidence.finding: Day-3 prisoner exchange remained in coordination phase; Ukraine had publicly submitted 1,000-name list; Putin blame-shifted by claiming Ukrainian unreadiness; Ukraine rebutted with public 'must take place' framing.

evidence.source: Euromaidan Press — Moscow's Victory Day ceasefire reduced combat but became Russia's stockpile window, ISW finds
evidence.finding: ISW retrospective finding (May 12 publication; analyzing in-window May 9-11 conduct): Russia used the ceasefire as a strategic stockpile window. Drone strikes rose during truce; Molniya drones pre-positioned and deployed; reserves repositioned forward in Lyman direction. Strategic-format-exploitation level bad-faith documented. Ukraine's sustained 3-day breach-free conduct is analytically deepened by contrast.
evidence.window_note: Published May 12 but analyzes in-window conduct; permitted per prompt's hard constraint.

evidence.source: Washington Times — Russia, Ukraine exchange blame for continued fighting under US-brokered ceasefire
evidence.finding: Day-3 mutual-blame framing attempted by Russia; Ukrainian transparency posture preserved; civilian-casualty documentation continued.
Multi-source documentation of day-3 close pattern.

evidence.source: Nation (Pakistan) — Ukraine, Russia trade accusations violating US-backed ceasefire
evidence.finding: Day-3 international media confirmation of mutual-blame attempt; independent reporting did not produce Ukraine-attributable breach evidence.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.0 sustained. A1 detection: Ukrainian General Staff and civil-defense reporting continued operational throughout day 3; A4 amplification: Zelenskyy public communications continued balanced acknowledgment (partial Russian compliance + continued strikes) across full 3-day window. No AWR-degrading conduct in window. No basis for dimensional movement (no new positive enacted detection-infrastructure change).
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.0 sustained. E1 affective resonance: state acknowledgment of Russian-caused civilian harm continued (May 11 Kharkiv/Kherson strikes documented within hours); E4 validation: no E-dimension degrading conduct by Ukraine. Civilian-protection posture maintained. No basis for dimensional movement.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 published score CONFIRMED. INTERNAL ASSESSMENT 4.0 (+0.5 dimensional credit awarded for cumulative 3-day cooperative-action follow-through: 1,000-prisoner list publicly submitted; 'must take place' framing rebutted counterparty blame-shifting; US-guaranteed framework engaged; defensive posture maintained without attributable Ukrainian breach across 3 days). The exchange did not complete by ceasefire expiry, but per scanner assessor note: 'prepared and submitted but not executed' constitutes partial ACT credit. Internal 4.0 anchors the dimensional upward pressure; published 3.5 retained at composite level due to sub-threshold composite delta.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.5 sustained. No EQ-relevant changes in window; structural pattern from prior cycles.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.0 sustained. B3 scope clarity: ceasefire-engagement framing maintained civilian-protection boundary across full 3-day window; B4 refusal ethics: Ukraine did not invoke disproportionate retaliation despite documented Russian civilian harm. No basis for dimensional movement absent new enacted scope-clarity action.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.0 sustained. AB2 transparency: balanced Zelenskyy reporting across all 3 days (Russian reduction + Russian violations + Ukrainian compliance status); General Staff clash counts publicly accessible; civilian casualty disclosure within hours. No basis for dimensional movement (transparency already at anchor-3 level).
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.0 sustained. S5 coalitional compassion: continued participation in US-brokered trilateral structure across full window; S2 long-term impact: 1,000-prisoner list submission represents structural cooperative-action commitment beyond a single-cycle gesture. No basis for dimensional movement absent post-ceasefire structural-policy action.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.0 published score CONFIRMED. INTERNAL ASSESSMENT 3.5 (+0.5 dimensional credit awarded for cumulative 3-day consistency-under-pressure: Ukraine maintained ceasefire engagement, prisoner-swap follow-through, transparency posture across the entire window despite documented counterparty breaches on all 3 days and ISW-confirmed counterparty strategic stockpile exploitation). The bad-faith-ceasefire inverted-signal rule applied evidence-by-evidence keeps the breach cost on Russia; Ukraine's 3-day sustained good-faith conduct earns dimensional credit. Internal 3.5 anchors the dimensional upward pressure; published 3.0 retained at composite level due to sub-threshold composite delta.

math_check.materiality_threshold_check: Delta +3.1 is SUB-THRESHOLD (below 5.0pt materiality floor for change-proposal generation). Confirm at 50.0.
math_check.validator_check: Reconstructs cleanly to 50.0 at published-dimensions level. Internal dimensions reconstruct cleanly to 53.1. Sub-threshold drift (3.1) does not trigger a change proposal. Functional band sustained at both internal and published levels.

trilateral_methodology_call_final: FINAL CEASEFIRE METHODOLOGY DECISION — DAY-3 SUSTAINED-CONDUCT DIMENSIONAL-CREDIT-UNDER-SUB-THRESHOLD-COMPOSITE RULE. The day-1 conditional credit (May 9) and day-2 sustained credit (May 10) are now resolved at day-3 close (May 11). Ukraine's sustained breach-free conduct across all 3 days + concrete cooperative-action follow-through (1,000-prisoner list submitted under counterparty obstruction) earns dimensional upward credit on ACT (+0.5) and INT (+0.5). Reconstructed internal composite 53.1; delta from published +3.1 is sub-threshold for change-proposal generation (5.0pt floor). Decision: CONFIRM at 50.0 / Functional with dimensional upward pressure carried forward. The rule is articulated explicitly for future reference: sustained breach-free conduct in counterparty-violated multi-day ceasefires earns dimensional credit but does not automatically produce material composite upgrades unless the credit accumulates to ≥5.0 composite points. This protects against composite churning while preserving the asymmetric-attribution principle.
negativeSignals: Active war context continues; civilian casualties from Russian strikes on day 3; prisoner exchange did not complete at expiry; coordination failure attribution disputed (Russia frames as Ukrainian unreadiness); post-ceasefire posture sustainability uncertain.
positiveSignals: 3-day sustained breach-free conduct record; 1,000-prisoner list publicly submitted; Zelenskyy 'must take place' framing publicly rebutted Putin's blame-shifting; US-guaranteed framework engaged; balanced public-reporting transparency posture maintained; counterparty's strategic stockpile-window exploitation (ISW finding) deepens by-contrast consistency of Ukrainian conduct. Sub-threshold dimensional upward pressure on ACT and INT carried forward.
sector_context: Eastern Europe / active-conflict cluster. The trilateral ceasefire window has CLOSED. Cross-reference: Russia (paired counterparty, floor sustained; 6-event cumulative breach record). The day-3 final decision establishes a precedent for future multi-day ceasefire scoring: dimensional credit is available for sustained good-faith conduct but composite movement is gated by the materiality threshold.
open_actions_resolved: Day-3 ceasefire conduct documented for Ukraine — breach-free across full 3-day window
1,000-prisoner list submission documented as concrete ACT signal — exchange execution failure attributable to coordination breakdown, not Ukrainian unreadiness
Day-3 sustained-conduct dimensional-credit-under-sub-threshold-composite rule articulated and applied
Internal ACT 3.5 → 4.0 and INT 3.0 → 3.5 documented as dimensional upward pressure
Bad-faith inverted-signal rule held across all 3 days without ambiguity
open_actions_carried: Watch: post-ceasefire posture in June rotation cycle (sustained good-faith conduct could consume the dimensional upward pressure into a material upgrade)
Watch: any post-ceasefire prisoner exchange completion (would convert the partial ACT credit to full execution credit)
Watch: any Ukrainian-attributed conduct that would invert the attribution rule

Dimensional upward pressure carry-forward: ACT 3.5 → 4.0 and INT 3.0 → 3.5 are documented for next material assessment; if positive trajectory continues, these become the floor of a potentially material upgrade
methodology_consistency_check: Day-3 final assessment applies the day-3 sustained-conduct dimensional-credit-under-sub-threshold-composite rule for the first time. The rule is consistent with methodology v1.2's evidence-by-evidence attribution principle (Russia's conduct registers against Russia; Ukraine's conduct credit is earned on Ukraine's own behavior, not by reference to Russia's behavior) and with the 5.0pt materiality threshold (small dimensional credits do not produce composite churning). The conservative-scoring rule from May 9 day-1 (conditional credit) and May 10 day-2 (sustained credit) is resolved at day-3 close by the materiality threshold — the credit is real and documented dimensionally, but is sub-threshold for change-proposal generation.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
